title: wedelia lehmannii
intro: Wedelia lehmannii, also known as the Mexican daisy or Flor de Muerto, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in Mexican and other Latin American cultures for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Wedelia lehmannii include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture for medicinal use, with some herbalists also recommending its use as a topical treatment for skin conditions.
